questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is popular ____ his colleagues.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is smarter than any student in the class.
options: She is the smartest than any student in the class.
She is smarter than any other student in the class.
She is smarter than any student in the class.
She is smarter than all student in the class.
questions.solution: She is smarter than any other student in the class.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The committee consists ____ ten members.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I have been working here from 2015.
options: I have been working here since 2015.
I have been working here from the year 2015.
I have been working here from 2015.
questions.solution: I have been working here since 2015.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is married ____ a mining engineer.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He asked that whether I could help him.
options: He asked that whether I could help him.
He asked if whether I could help him.
He asked that I could help him.
He asked whether I could help him.
questions.solution: He asked whether I could help him.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The manager asked ____ the report was ready.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He told the news to his brother and I.
options: He told the news to I and my brother.
He told the news to his brother and me.
He told the news to his brother and I.
He told the news to my brother and myself.
questions.solution: He told the news to his brother and me.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She was accused ____ stealing company data.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
options: The sceneries of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains have been breathtaking.
questions.solution: The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The project was completed ____ schedule.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I am looking forward to meet you.
options: I am looking forward for meeting you.
I look forward to meet you.
I am looking forward to meeting you.
I am looking forward to meet you.
questions.solution: I am looking forward to meeting you.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The company is committed ____ improving safety.

options: The committee have submitted its report.
The committee has submitted their report.
The committee has submitted its report.
The committee have submitted their report.
questions.solution: The committee has submitted its report.

questions.stem: He prefers coffee ____ tea in the morning.

options: He is more smarter than his brother.
He is smart than his brother.
He is smartest than his brother.
He is smarter than his brother.
questions.solution: He is smarter than his brother.

questions.stem: Rearrange: workers / demanding / are / wages / better / for
options: Workers are demanding for better wages
For better wages workers are demanding
Demanding are workers better for wages
Better wages for workers are demanding
questions.solution: Workers are demanding for better wages

questions.stem: A source has Vth = 12 V and Rth = 5 Ω. The maximum power deliverable to a matched load is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Synchronous condenser:
options: Over-excited synchronous motor for PF correction
questions.solution: Synchronous condenser: runs at no-load, over-excited provides leading VARs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing an ideal and a real silicon diode in reverse bias, which is correct?
options: The ideal diode carries more reverse current.
The real diode carries a small reverse leakage current while the ideal one carries none.
questions.solution: An ideal diode blocks perfectly in reverse; a real diode has a small temperature-dependent leakage (saturation) current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Single-phase half-wave rectifier with R load:
questions.solution: Half-wave: conducts only in positive half cycle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method on a balanced 3-phase load, the two readings are equal when the power factor is:
questions.solution: At unity pf the phase angle is zero and both wattmeters read equal positive power; at pf 0.5 one reads zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DC generator types:
questions.solution: Compound: cumulatively (over-compound, flat, under-compound) or differentially.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SI unit of luminous flux is the:
questions.solution: Luminous flux is measured in lumens; illuminance (lumen/m²) is the lux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an ideal buck (step-down) DC–DC converter in continuous conduction, the output voltage is:
questions.solution: A buck converter gives Vo = D·Vs; the boost converter gives Vs/(1−D).

questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 800 W and W₂ = 300 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 800 + 300 = 1100 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two identical resistors are connected first in series and then in parallel across the same battery. Which is correct?
options: The series combination dissipates more power.
The parallel combination dissipates four times the power of the series combination.
questions.solution: Series R_eq = 2R (P = V²/2R); parallel R_eq = R/2 (P = 2V²/R) — four times greater for the same voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series RLC circuit has R = 5 Ω, L = 0.1 H, C = 100 µF. Its quality factor Q is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Substitution theorem:
options: Any branch can be replaced without affecting other currents/voltages
questions.solution: If branch voltage/current remains same, the substitution is valid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Commutation:
options: Process of reversing armature coil current during commutation
questions.solution: Poor commutation → sparking. Causes: reactance voltage, armature reaction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Oscilloscope bandwidth:
questions.solution: Rise time: t_r = 0.35/BW. Sampling: ≥ 2.5× BW for digital scope.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RTD:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The back EMF of a DC motor is:
questions.solution: Eb = (PφZN)/(60A); it rises with speed and flux and opposes the supply.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 150Ω and 22Ω in parallel. Current through 22Ω if total I=0.16A:
questions.solution: I_R2 = V/R2. In parallel, voltage same across both resistors.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cage vs wound rotor:
options: Wound rotor allows external resistance for starting
questions.solution: Wound rotor: slip rings + external R for high starting torque.
